title: Correlation wheel plots with circlecorR
text: traditional correlation matrix carries large amount redundant information. kk variables k2k^2 cells, k(k−1)/2k(k-1)/2 unique: diagonal self-correlations (r=1r = 1), two triangles mirror , large blocks within-category correlations often irrelevant, contribute reduced statistical power. kk grows, interesting -category relationships buried redundancy figure becomes unreadable. correlation wheel introduced Gharibans et al first clinical paper show spatial dysrhythmias measured body surface, correlated gastroduodenal symptoms (Gharibans et al. 2019). correlation wheel shows variables periphery circle, grouped category, correlations interest drawn curved links coloured strength coefficient, filtered based custom statistical significance thresholds. circlecorR reproduces features generate correlation wheel plot using standard patient-per-row type dataframes R, optionality customise grouping, colours, statistics.

title: Correlation wheel plots with circlecorR
text: (CRAN release pending.) Dependencies (circlize psych) install automatically command .
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("kriz98/circlecorR", build_vignettes = TRUE)

what: Quick start: straight from your data
title: Correlation wheel plots with circlecorR
text: circlecorR designed work straight data frame – one row per subject one column per variable – separate step build correlation matrices . Hand data frame directly corr_wheel() computes correlations (p-values) . thing supply groups: named list mapping category variables. selects variables appear (columns, ID, ignored) sets order around wheel. homage Gharibans et al seminal paper, use synthetic gastro_symptoms dataset throughout vignette (see ?gastro_symptoms exact shape). synthetic example dataset bundled package. Swap data frame (one row per subject) use data. notable papers figures used include: Gharibans AA, Coleman TP, Mousa H, Kunkel DC. Spatial patterns high-resolution electrogastrography correlate severity symptoms patients functional dyspepsia gastroparesis. Clin Gastroenterol Hepatol. 2019 Dec;17(13):2668–77. Gharibans AA, Calder S, Varghese C, Waite S, Schamberg G, Daker C, et al. Gastric dysfunction patients chronic nausea vomiting syndromes defined noninvasive gastric mapping device. Sci Transl Med. 2022 Sept 21;14(663):eabq3544. Wang TH-H, Varghese C, Calder S, Gharibans , Schamberg G, Bartlett , et al. Long-term evaluation gastric electrophysiology, symptoms quality life pancreaticoduodenectomy. HPB (Oxford). 2025 Dec;27(12):1535–42. Xu W, Wang T, Foong D, Schamberg G, Evennett N, Beban G, et al. Characterization gastric dysfunction fundoplication using body surface gastric mapping. J Gastrointest Surg. 2024 Mar;28(3):236–45. Xu W, Gharibans AA, Calder S, Schamberg G, Walters , Jang J, et al. Defining phenotyping gastric abnormalities long-term type 1 diabetes using novel body surface gastric mapping device. Gastro Hep Adv. 2023 Aug 18;2(8):1120–32.
code: groups <- list(   Demographics = c("Age", "BMI"),   Metrics      = c("Amplitude", "Fed-Fasted AR", "Frequency", "GA-RI"),   Symptoms     = c("Nausea", "Early satiety", "Bloating",                    "Upper GI pain", "Lower GI pain", "Heartburn"),   Scores       = c("GCSI", "PAGI-SYM", "PAGI-QoL", "EQ-5D") ) # `gastro_symptoms` is a synthetic per-row example dataset shipped with the package head(gastro_symptoms[, 1:5]) #>   Age  BMI Amplitude Fed-Fasted AR Frequency #> 1  74 41.0  30.26932     2.0004345  2.808546 #> 2  51 42.8  29.15670     0.7884043  1.760702 #> 3  59 36.2  29.73068     0.1899037  3.483917 #> 4  47 40.8  31.16330    -0.8294263  2.842009 #> 5  29 46.1  31.07111     0.9579552  4.015299 #> 6  50 36.4  29.86780    -2.0601868  1.179358  corr_wheel(   gastro_symptoms,             # raw data: one row per subject   groups      = groups,   method      = "pearson",     # correlation method   adjust      = "hochberg",    # multiple-comparison adjustment (see below)   sig_level   = 0.05,          # hide links with adjusted p > 0.05   r_threshold = 0.3,           # ...and links with |r| < 0.3   r_limits    = c(-0.6, 0.6) )

title: Correlation wheel plots with circlecorR
text: Two wheel’s important features never draws self-correlations (diagonal) , default, hides within-category correlations (hide_within_group = TRUE). removes redundant parts matrix leaves -category structure. Crucially, carried statistics. Multiple-comparison correction penalises number hypotheses tested. self- within-category correlations never tested, count towards family. corr_wheel() therefore applies adjustment correlations displays. Shrinking family makes correction less severe, thus improving power, remaining statistically consistent shown.  family smaller, raw p-value corrected smaller factor. Taking Bonferroni transparent example, correlation penalised number tests family – 92 rather 120: step-methods ("holm", "hochberg", "BH") behave way. want correlation p-value matrices , table, report, purpose – compute_correlations() function corr_wheel() calls internally, available :
code:
res <- corr_wheel(gastro_symptoms, groups = groups, adjust = "hochberg",                   r_threshold = 0.3, r_limits = c(-0.6, 0.6)) k <- length(unlist(groups)) cat("Unique correlations in the full matrix:", k * (k - 1) / 2, "
") #> Unique correlations in the full matrix: 120 cat("Correlations actually tested (the family):", res$n_tests, "
") #> Correlations actually tested (the family): 92 p_raw <- 5e-4                       # a raw p-value for one correlation k_all <- k * (k - 1) / 2  cat("Bonferroni across the full matrix:", signif(p_raw * k_all, 3), "
") #> Bonferroni across the full matrix: 0.06 cat("Bonferroni across the family only:", signif(p_raw * res$n_tests, 3), "
") #> Bonferroni across the family only: 0.046 cc <- compute_correlations(gastro_symptoms, method = "pearson") str(cc) #> List of 4 #>  $ r     : num [1:16, 1:16] 1 0.000715 -0.094705 -0.208429 -0.007022 ... #>   ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. ..$ : chr [1:16] "Age" "BMI" "Amplitude" "Fed-Fasted AR" ... #>   .. ..$ : chr [1:16] "Age" "BMI" "Amplitude" "Fed-Fasted AR" ... #>  $ p     : num [1:16, 1:16] 0 0.9944 0.3486 0.0374 0.9447 ... #>   ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. ..$ : chr [1:16] "Age" "BMI" "Amplitude" "Fed-Fasted AR" ... #>   .. ..$ : chr [1:16] "Age" "BMI" "Amplitude" "Fed-Fasted AR" ... #>  $ n     : num 100 #>  $ method: chr "pearson" #>  - attr(*, "class")= chr "circlecor"

title: Correlation wheel plots with circlecorR
text: scheme sets category colours diverging link palette together, one named preset. Built-options listed corr_wheel_schemes(): "default" – seaborn-like categorical palette blue-white-red diverging scale used throughout vignette. "colorblind" – Okabe-Ito categorical palette colourblind-safe (PuOr) diverging scale. "ocean" – hue-varied cool tones (teal, blue, indigo, violet, slate) categories, blue--warm diverging scale. "vivid" – brighter, higher-contrast categorical palette, still paired blue-white-red diverging scale. "alimetry" – blues alternating warm golds, matching blue--yellow diverging scale.   corr_wheel_scheme() returns scheme’s definition can start preset tweak – , lightening midpoint diverging scale:  build one entirely scratch passing list(colors = , palette = ) directly – colors unnamed vector cycled across however many categories , palette length-3 diverging scale (negative, midpoint, positive):
code: corr_wheel_schemes() #> [1] "default"    "colorblind" "ocean"      "vivid"      "alimetry" corr_wheel(gastro_symptoms, groups = groups, r_threshold = 0.3,           r_limits = c(-0.6, 0.6), scheme = "colorblind") corr_wheel(gastro_symptoms, groups = groups, r_threshold = 0.3,           r_limits = c(-0.6, 0.6), scheme = "alimetry") s <- corr_wheel_scheme("ocean") s$palette[2] <- "grey96" corr_wheel(gastro_symptoms, groups = groups, r_threshold = 0.3,           r_limits = c(-0.6, 0.6), scheme = s) corr_wheel(gastro_symptoms, groups = groups, r_threshold = 0.3,           r_limits = c(-0.6, 0.6),           scheme = list(colors = c("#7B2CBF", "#2A9D8F", "#E76F51", "#264653"),                        palette = c("#2A9D8F", "white", "#E76F51")))

title: Correlation wheel plots with circlecorR
text: colors maps categories colours, layered top scheme (default palette scheme NULL) – categories name overridden. labels gives pretty display names. named vectors – specify ones want change.
code: corr_wheel(   gastro_symptoms, groups = groups, r_threshold = 0.3, r_limits = c(-0.6, 0.6),   scheme = "colorblind",   colors = c(Scores = "black"),          # override just one category   labels = c("GA-RI" = "Rhythm index") )

title: Correlation wheel plots with circlecorR
text: tile_height – radial thickness category blocks (smaller = thinner). link_lwd – line width links (larger = thicker).
code: corr_wheel(   gastro_symptoms, groups = groups, r_threshold = 0.3, r_limits = c(-0.6, 0.6),   tile_height = 0.12,   # thicker blocks   link_lwd    = 3       # thicker lines )

title: Correlation wheel plots with circlecorR
text: palette sets three colours c(-limit, 0, +limit) (overriding scheme’s) r_limits scale range.
code: corr_wheel(   gastro_symptoms, groups = groups, r_threshold = 0.3,   palette  = c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"),   # blue - white - red   r_limits = c(-0.5, 0.5) )

title: Correlation wheel plots with circlecorR
text: corr_wheel() draws active graphics device, save usual way:
code: png("correlation_wheel.png", width = 2500, height = 2000, res = 300) corr_wheel(gastro_symptoms, groups = groups, r_threshold = 0.3,            r_limits = c(-0.6, 0.6)) dev.off()

title: Correlation wheel plots with circlecorR
text: Everything covered , glance: corr_wheel() returns (invisibly) ordered variables, resolved group colour maps, colour function, masked matrix actually plotted – handy reproducibility building caption.

title: Circular Correlation Wheel Plots
text: Circular correlation “wheel” plots native R. circlecorR draws correlation wheel plots variables arranged around ring, grouped colour-tiled category, connected curved links whose colour maps correlation coefficient using circlize. traditional correlation matrix mostly redundant. diagonal self-correlations, two mirror-image triangles, blocks within-category correlations bury -category relationships care . wheel, first seen Gharibans et al. (2019), keeps relationships interest. Hiding self- within-category correlations also removes multiple-comparison family, improves statistical power (see vignette("circlecorR")).

title: Circular Correlation Wheel Plots
text: (CRAN release pending.) Dependencies (circlize psych) install automatically command .
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("kriz98/circlecorR", build_vignettes = TRUE)

what: Quick start — straight from your data
title: Circular Correlation Wheel Plots
text: circlecorR designed work straight data frame, one row per subject, one column per variable, separate step build correlation matrices . Hand data frame corr_wheel() computes correlations p-values . thing supply groups, selects variables orders around wheel (extra columns like IDs ignored).
code: library(circlecorR)  # `gastro_symptoms` is a synthetic example dataset bundled with the package -- # swap it for your own data frame (one row per subject) to use your own data. groups <- list(   Demographics = c("Age", "BMI"),   Metrics      = c("Amplitude", "Fed-Fasted AR", "Frequency", "GA-RI"),   Symptoms     = c("Nausea", "Early satiety", "Bloating",                    "Upper GI pain", "Lower GI pain", "Heartburn"),   Scores       = c("GCSI", "PAGI-SYM", "PAGI-QoL", "EQ-5D") )  corr_wheel(   gastro_symptoms,          # one row per subject -- use your own data here   groups      = groups,   method      = "pearson",   adjust      = "hochberg",   sig_level   = 0.05,   r_threshold = 0.3,   r_limits    = c(-0.6, 0.6) )

title: Circular Correlation Wheel Plots
text: link variables j drawn hold: different categories (hide_within_group = TRUE), never variable (self-correlations always excluded); p_adjusted <= sig_level; |r| >= r_threshold. correlations left step 1 form family comparisons. adjust correction applied family — redundant self- within-category correlations don’t inflate count, power improves. corr_wheel() returns n_tests (family size) family-adjusted p-matrix reporting.

title: Circular Correlation Wheel Plots
text: correlation wheel introduced : Gharibans AA, Coleman TP, Mousa H, Kunkel DC. Spatial patterns high-resolution electrogastrography correlate severity symptoms patients functional dyspepsia gastroparesis. Clin Gastroenterol Hepatol. 2019 Dec;17(13):2668–77. since used several gastric physiology studies: Gharibans AA, Calder S, Varghese C, Waite S, Schamberg G, Daker C, et al. HPB (Oxford). 2025 Dec;27(12):1535–42. Xu W, Wang T, Foong D, Schamberg G, Evennett N, Beban G, et al. Characterization gastric dysfunction fundoplication using body surface gastric mapping. J Gastrointest Surg. 2024 Mar;28(3):236–45. Xu W, Gharibans AA, Calder S, Schamberg G, Walters , Jang J, et al. Defining phenotyping gastric abnormalities long-term type 1 diabetes using novel body surface gastric mapping device. Gastro Hep Adv. 2023 Aug 18;2(8):1120–32.

what: circlecorR: Circular Correlation Wheel Plots — circlecorR-package
title: circlecorR: Circular Correlation Wheel Plots — circlecorR-package
text: Draws MNE-style circular "correlation wheel" plots natively R. main entry point corr_wheel(); compute_correlations() builds r/p input raw data.

title: circlecorR: Circular Correlation Wheel Plots — circlecorR-package
text: Maintainer: Chris Varghese chris.varghese@auckland.ac.nz Authors: Chris Varghese chris.varghese@auckland.ac.nz Armen . Gharibans

what: Compute a correlation matrix and (raw) p-values from raw data — compute_correlations
title: Compute a correlation matrix and (raw) p-values from raw data — compute_correlations
text: thin wrapper around psych::corr.test() returns correlation (r) unadjusted p-value (p) matrices bundled single object suitable passing straight corr_wheel().

title: Compute a correlation matrix and (raw) p-values from raw data — compute_correlations
code: compute_correlations(   data,   vars = NULL,   method = c("pearson", "spearman", "kendall"),   use = "pairwise.complete.obs" )

title: Compute a correlation matrix and (raw) p-values from raw data — compute_correlations
text: data data frame matrix observations (rows) variables (columns). vars Optional character vector selecting ordering columns data use. Defaults columns. method Correlation method, passed psych::corr.test(). One "pearson", "spearman", "kendall". use Handling missing values, passed psych::corr.test().

title: Compute a correlation matrix and (raw) p-values from raw data — compute_correlations
text: object class "circlecor": list elements r (correlation matrix), p (symmetric matrix raw, unadjusted p-values), n (pairwise sample sizes psych), method.

title: Compute a correlation matrix and (raw) p-values from raw data — compute_correlations
text: Multiple-comparison adjustment deliberately applied . correlation wheel, self-correlations (usually) within-category correlations never tested, count towards family comparisons. corr_wheel() therefore applies adjustment , exactly set correlations displays – statistically consistent powerful adjusting across full matrix. See adjust hide_within_group arguments corr_wheel().

title: Compute a correlation matrix and (raw) p-values from raw data — compute_correlations
code: cc <- compute_correlations(mtcars, method = "pearson") str(cc) #> List of 4 #>  $ r     : num [1:11, 1:11] 1 -0.852 -0.848 -0.776 0.681 ... #>   ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. ..$ : chr [1:11] "mpg" "cyl" "disp" "hp" ... #>   .. ..$ : chr [1:11] "mpg" "cyl" "disp" "hp" ... #>  $ p     : num [1:11, 1:11] 0.00 6.11e-10 9.38e-10 1.79e-07 1.78e-05 ... #>   ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. ..$ : chr [1:11] "mpg" "cyl" "disp" "hp" ... #>   .. ..$ : chr [1:11] "mpg" "cyl" "disp" "hp" ... #>  $ n     : num 32 #>  $ method: chr "pearson" #>  - attr(*, "class")= chr "circlecor"

what: Draw a circular correlation wheel plot — corr_wheel
title: Draw a circular correlation wheel plot — corr_wheel
text: Arranges variables around circle, grouped colour-tiled category, connects curved links whose colour maps correlation coefficient. Non-significant, weak, (optionally) within-category correlations masked. reproduces MNE-style "connectivity circle" natively R using circlize.

title: Draw a circular correlation wheel plot — corr_wheel
code: corr_wheel(   data,   groups = NULL,   scheme = NULL,   colors = NULL,   labels = NULL,   order = NULL,   sig_level = 0.05,   r_threshold = 0,   hide_within_group = TRUE,   method = c("pearson", "spearman", "kendall"),   adjust = "holm",   use = "pairwise.complete.obs",   r_limits = c(-0.5, 0.5),   palette = NULL,   start_degree = 90,   group_gap = 5,   node_gap = 1.5,   link_lwd = 1.6,   sort_links = TRUE,   tile_height = 0.06,   label_cex = 0.85,   label_pad = 0.45,   label_r_offset = 0.07,   tile_border = "white",   title = NULL,   legend = TRUE,   colorbar = TRUE,   legend_title = "Category",   colorbar_title = "Correlation\ncoefficient" )

title: Draw a circular correlation wheel plot — corr_wheel
text: data data frame matrix one row per observation (e.g. one row per patient) variables columns. Correlations p-values computed via compute_correlations() using method, adjust, use. groups supplied, variables names used (order), extra columns ID simply ignored. groups Category assignment variables. Either named vector (variable = category) named list (category = c(variables)). order categories sets order around wheel. NULL, variables share one group. scheme colour scheme providing base category colours diverging link palette together. One : NULL (default) – use package default scheme; built-scheme name, see corr_wheel_schemes() (e.g. "colorblind", "ocean", "vivid", "alimetry"); custom list(colors = , palette = ), returned corr_wheel_scheme() (optionally tweaked). colors palette (), supplied, override scheme's corresponding piece – can pick scheme still tweak one category's colour, instance. colors Named vector mapping category colour, layered top scheme (default palette scheme NULL). categories name overridden; others keep scheme's colour. labels Named vector mapping variable name display label. Missing entries fall back variable name. order Optional character vector giving explicit variable order around wheel. Overrides ordering groups. Must contain every variable. sig_level Significance threshold; links adjusted p > sig_level hidden. r_threshold Minimum absolute correlation display. hide_within_group Logical; TRUE (default) correlations two variables category hidden – excluded multiple-comparison family (see Details). Self-correlations (diagonal) always excluded. method, use Passed compute_correlations(): correlation method missing-value handling. adjust Multiple-comparison adjustment method applied raw p-values displayed family correlations (see Details). method accepted stats::p.adjust() (e.g. "holm", "hochberg", "BH", "bonferroni", "none"). r_limits Length-2 numeric giving colour-scale limits (c(vmin, vmax)). Correlations beyond clamped colour. palette Colours diverging link scale c(r_limits[1], midpoint, r_limits[2]), overriding scheme's. NULL (default) uses scheme's palette, blue-white-red scale scheme also NULL. start_degree Angle (degrees) first variable; 90 places top, going clockwise. group_gap, node_gap Gaps (degrees) categories variables within category. link_lwd Line width (size) links. Increase thicker lines. sort_links TRUE (default), stronger correlations drawn last (top). tile_height Radial thickness (size) coloured category blocks. Smaller values give thinner blocks rim. label_cex, tile_border Label text size tile border colour. label_pad Extra canvas padding (fraction circle radius) keep long outer labels clipped. Increase longer labels. label_r_offset Radial gap (circle-radius units) outer edge tiles start labels. title Optional plot title. legend, colorbar Logical toggles category legend correlation colour bar. legend_title, colorbar_title Titles legend colour bar.

title: Draw a circular correlation wheel plot — corr_wheel
text: Invisibly, list ordered vars, resolved groups, colors, col_fun colour mapping, masked matrix correlations actually drawn (others NA), family-adjusted p-value matrix p_adjusted, number drawn links n_links, size comparison family n_tests, adjust method applied.

title: Draw a circular correlation wheel plot — corr_wheel
text: traditional correlation matrix dominated redundant information: diagonal self-correlations, mirror-image lower triangle, blocks within-category correlations rarely interest. wheel keeps correlations actually want inspect – default -category correlations, self- within-category correlations hidden. display choice; carried statistics. multiple-comparison adjustment (adjust) applied family correlations shown – .e. excluding self- , hide_within_group = TRUE, within-category correlations. redundant comparisons longer count towards family, correction less severe statistical power improves.

title: Draw a circular correlation wheel plot — corr_wheel
code: grp <- list(   Demographics = c("Age", "BMI"),   Metrics      = c("Amplitude", "Fed-Fasted AR", "Frequency", "GA-RI"),   Symptoms     = c("Nausea", "Early satiety", "Bloating", "Upper GI pain",                    "Lower GI pain", "Heartburn"),   Scores       = c("GCSI", "PAGI-SYM", "PAGI-QoL", "EQ-5D") )  # `gastro_symptoms` is a synthetic example dataset bundled with the package # (available directly after library(circlecorR) -- no need to call data()). corr_wheel(gastro_symptoms, groups = grp, r_threshold = 0.3,            r_limits = c(-0.6, 0.6))   # A built-in colour scheme, with one category colour overridden corr_wheel(gastro_symptoms, groups = grp, r_threshold = 0.3,            scheme = "colorblind", colors = c(Scores = "black"))

what: Get a built-in colour scheme — corr_wheel_scheme
title: Get a built-in colour scheme — corr_wheel_scheme
text: Returns category-colour diverging-link-palette definition built-scheme, can inspect tweak copy passing corr_wheel()'s scheme argument.

title: Get a built-in colour scheme — corr_wheel_scheme
code: corr_wheel_scheme(name = "default")

title: Get a built-in colour scheme — corr_wheel_scheme
text: name scheme name; see corr_wheel_schemes() available options.

title: Get a built-in colour scheme — corr_wheel_scheme
text: list elements colors (unnamed vector category colours, cycled across however many categories plotted) palette (length-3 diverging colour vector: negative, midpoint, positive).

title: Get a built-in colour scheme — corr_wheel_scheme
code: s <- corr_wheel_scheme("colorblind") s$palette #> [1] "#E66101" "#F7F7F7" "#5E3C99"  # Tweak a copy and use it s$palette[2] <- "grey95" data(gastro_symptoms) grp <- list(Demographics = c("Age", "BMI"),            Metrics = c("Amplitude", "Fed-Fasted AR", "Frequency", "GA-RI")) if (requireNamespace("psych", quietly = TRUE)) {   corr_wheel(gastro_symptoms, groups = grp, scheme = s, r_threshold = 0) }

title: List built-in colour schemes — corr_wheel_schemes
text: Returns names colour schemes bundled package, use scheme argument corr_wheel().

title: List built-in colour schemes — corr_wheel_schemes
code: corr_wheel_schemes()

title: List built-in colour schemes — corr_wheel_schemes
text: character vector scheme names.

title: List built-in colour schemes — corr_wheel_schemes
code: corr_wheel_schemes() #> [1] "default"    "colorblind" "ocean"      "vivid"      "alimetry"

title: Synthetic gastric-symptom dataset — gastro_symptoms
text: fully simulated dataset (patient data) mimicking structure gastric-symptom study: demographics, gastric metrics, symptom sub-scales, summary scores 100 imaginary subjects, one row per subject. Loadings deliberately moderate uneven, realistic subset correlations significant. Generated data-raw/make_example_data.R.

title: Synthetic gastric-symptom dataset — gastro_symptoms
code: gastro_symptoms

title: Synthetic gastric-symptom dataset — gastro_symptoms
text: data frame 100 rows 16 variables: Age, BMI Demographics. Amplitude, Fed-Fasted AR, Frequency, GA-RI Simulated gastric metrics. Nausea, Early satiety, Bloating, Upper GI pain, Lower GI pain, Heartburn Symptom sub-scale scores. GCSI, PAGI-SYM, PAGI-QoL, EQ-5D Summary symptom quality--life scores.

title: Synthetic gastric-symptom dataset — gastro_symptoms
text: Bundled package (LazyData: true), available directly gastro_symptoms soon library(circlecorR) – data() call needed. Pass straight corr_wheel(); see vignette("circlecorR") worked example.
